title: carya ovata var. australis
intro: Carya ovata var. australis, also known as the southern black walnut, is a deciduous tree native to eastern North America. The southern black walnut is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The tree's bark, leaves, and seeds have been used in herbal preparations to promote digestive health, reduce pain, and support immune function, among other benefits. Southern black walnut has been used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties.
